title: oscbank~

flags.name: -partial <list>
flags.description:
sets list of partials for all oscillators
default: all 1

flags.name: -amp <list>
flags.description:
sets list of amplitudes for all oscillators
default: all 1

flags.name: -phase <list>
flags.description:
sets list of phases for all oscillators
default: all 0

flags.name: -ramp <list>
flags.description: sets list of ramp times for all oscillators

flags.name: -freq <float>
flags.description:
sets fundamental frequency in Hz
default: 0

flags.name: -rampall <float>
flags.description:
sets a ramp time for all oscillators
default: all 10

methods.description: list of partials for all oscillators
list of amplitudes for all oscillators
list of phases (0-1) for all oscillators
list of ramp time for all oscillators
sets ramp time for all oscillators

body: [oscbank~] is a bank made of [sine~] objects. You can set any number of oscillators and control their parameters. Unlike [oscbank2~], you have a fundamental frequency input and the frequency of each oscillator is specified as a ratio of that frequency. If you use flags, the number of elements in the list (such as amplitude list) sets the number of oscillators in the bank, and you must not use regular arguments in this case.
